title: begonia axillaris
intro: Begonia axillaris, also known as Begonia, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and wounds. The health benefits of Begonia axillaris include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and promoting wound healing, which can be attributed to its active compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids. In herbal preparations, Begonia axillaris is often used in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, and it can also be applied topically in the form of creams and salves to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
